story.iWant: Postman environment variables for base URL and auth tokens
story.soThat: I can easily switch between environments (dev, staging, production)
story.tasks: Task 1: Extend PostmanCollectionService for Environment Generation (AC: 1, 2) Task 2: Add Environment Endpoints (AC: 1, 3) Task 3: Configure Environment-Specific Values (AC: 2, 3) Task 4: Mark Variables as Sensitive (AC: 4) Task 5: Update Documentation (AC: 5) Task 6: Integration Testing (AC: 1, 3, 4) Task 7: Manual Verification (AC: 4, 5)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. Postman environment JSON generated alongside collection - Environment JSON files created using Postman Collection Format v2.1.0, Generated automatically when accessing /api/docs/postman endpoint, Separate environment files for dev, staging, and production environments, Compatible with Postman Collection v2.1.0 specification 2. Environment variables defined - baseUrl: API base URL dynamically configured from environment, authToken: JWT bearer token placeholder for authentication, apiKey: API key placeholder (if applicable for future endpoints), All variables properly typed and validated 3. Separate environment files generated - api-environment-dev.postman_environment.json - Development environment configuration, api-environment-staging.postman_environment.json - Staging environment configuration, api-environment-prod.postman_environment.json - Production environment configuration, Each environment contains appropriate values for that environment 4. Variables marked as sensitive - authToken marked as sensitive in Postman (hidden value), apiKey marked as sensitive in Postman (hidden value), baseUrl marked as non-sensitive (visible for easy switching), Proper variable type configuration (text, hidden, default) 5. Environment selection guide in README - Documentation explaining how to import and use environment files, Step-by-step instructions for switching between environments, Environment-specific configuration notes, Troubleshooting guide for common issues

doc.title: Epic 15 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Epic 15, mevcut Swagger/OpenAPI 3.0 documentation'dan (Epic 8) otomatik olarak Postman Collection'ları generate eder.

doc.title: Epic 15 Story Breakdown
doc.snippet: As a developer, I want Postman environment variables for base URL and auth tokens, So that I can easily switch between environments.

doc.title: Story 15.1 Previous Implementation
doc.section: Dev Agent Record - Completion Notes List
doc.snippet: Package: openapi-to-postmanv2, Service: Full PostmanCollectionService implementation with validation and retry, Controller: DocsController with GET /api/docs/postman endpoint

doc.title: Testing Strategy
doc.snippet: Test coverage requirements: ≥90% for all service methods and controllers

file.reason: Core service to extend with environment generation methods - contains generateCollectionFromSwagger(), getCollectionJson(), validateOpenApiSpec(), fetchOpenApiSpec()
Interface definition to extend for environment generation - includes service contract for Postman collection operations
Controller to extend with new endpoint GET /api/docs/postman/environment/:env - follows same pattern as GET /api/docs/postman
Module configuration for dependency injection - may need updates if new services added

ecosystem.name: node
ecosystem.package: Core NestJS framework - dependency injection, controllers, decorators
Express platform adapter - HTTP server integration
Official Postman converter for OpenAPI specs - used for collection generation

story-context.constraints: Must follow Postman Collection Format v2.1.0 specification for environment JSON structure Environment generation must build on existing PostmanCollectionService from Story 15-1 (no recreation) Sensitive variables (authToken, apiKey) must be marked with type: 'hidden' in Postman baseUrl must include /api path for all environments Service methods must be extendable following existing interface patterns Endpoint must follow RESTful design with proper HTTP status codes and error handling Test coverage must meet ≥90% standard established in Epic 9 Must integrate with Epic 8 Swagger infrastructure (GET /api/docs-json endpoint) File naming convention: kebab-case for files, PascalCase for interfaces Dependency injection must be properly configured through NestJS module system

interface.name: IPostmanCollectionService
interface.signature: interface IPostmanCollectionService { generateCollectionFromSwagger(): Promise<PostmanCollection> getCollectionJson(): Promise<Buffer> validateOpenApiSpec(spec: any): OpenApiValidationResult fetchOpenApiSpec(): Promise<any> postProcessCollection(collection: PostmanCollection): PostmanCollection }

interface.name: PostmanEnvironmentService
interface.signature: GET /api/docs/postman/environment/:env Path parameter: env (dev|staging|prod) Response: application/json - downloadable environment JSON file Headers: Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="api-environment-${env}.postman_environment.json"

interface.name: PostmanEnvironment

tests.standards: Follow Epic 9 testing strategy with ≥90% coverage. Use Jest testing framework. Create unit tests for service methods and integration tests for controller endpoints. Follow patterns established in Story 15-1 (src/common/postman/__tests__/postman-collection.service.spec.ts, test/integration/docs/postman-collection.integration-spec.ts). Mock external dependencies including openapi-to-postmanv2 package.

ideas.test: Test environment JSON generation with valid Postman v2.1.0 format Validate file structure: id, name, values array Verify compatibility with Postman Collection v2.1.0 specification
Test generateEnvironment() method produces correct variables Verify baseUrl, authToken, apiKey are defined Test variable typing and validation logic
Test all three environment types (dev, staging, prod) Verify each environment produces separate JSON file Validate environment-specific baseUrl values
Test variable sensitivity configuration Verify authToken and apiKey marked as type: 'hidden' Verify baseUrl marked as type: 'text'

Test GET /api/docs/postman/environment/:env endpoints Validate all three environment endpoints work correctly Test error handling for invalid environment parameter Verify download headers are set correctly
